name: singleton_set_unique_elt_external_body
code:
proof fn phi_1_singleton_set_unique_elt_external_body(s: Set<nat>, a: nat, b: nat)
    requires
        s.finite(),
        s.len() == 1,
        s.contains(a),
        s.contains(b),
    ensures
        a == b,
{
    singleton_set_unique_elt(s, a, b);
}
reason: `singleton_set_unique_elt` is `external_body` — the fundamental uniqueness property of singleton sets is trusted without proof; if wrong, `set_mismatch` inherits unsoundness

name: set_mismatch_derives_false
code:
proof fn phi_2_set_mismatch_derives_false(s1: Set<nat>, s2: Set<nat>, missing: nat)
    requires
        s1.finite(),
        s2.finite(),
        s1.len() == s2.len(),
        forall |elt| s2.contains(elt) ==> s1.contains(elt),
        s1.contains(missing),
        !s2.contains(missing),
    ensures
        false,
{
    set_mismatch(s1, s2, missing);
}
reason: `set_mismatch` derives `false` — a contradiction lemma whose correctness depends on the external_body `singleton_set_unique_elt`; the chain of trust bottoms out at an unverified axiom

name: singleton_any_two_elements_equal
reason: The external_body axiom allows deriving that any concrete singleton set has exactly one element — testing the axiom on a concrete set to confirm it doesn't over-constrain

name: set_mismatch_len_zero
reason: A set of length 0 containing an element is contradictory — tests whether `set_mismatch` can be invoked at the degenerate boundary where `s2` is empty

name: set_mismatch_strict_subset_equal_len
reason: A strict subset of a finite set cannot have equal cardinality — tests the consistency of `set_mismatch`'s preconditions against basic set theory
